name: Marty Paich
description: American pianist, composer, arranger, record producer, music director and bandleader (1925-1995)
abstract: Martin Louis Paich (January 23, 1925 – August 12, 1995) was an American pianist, composer, arranger, record producer, music director, and conductor. As a musician and arranger he worked with jazz musicians Peggy Lee, Ella Fitzgerald, Stan Kenton, Al Hirt, Art Pepper, Buddy Rich, Ray Brown, Shorty Rogers, Pete Rugolo, Ray Charles and Mel …

about_quotes.speaker: Manny Albam
about_quotes.evidence: I didn’t meet the man until much later, maybe about six years ago I met him in California... I had never met him and we had breakfast together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Manny Albam interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Sheldon
about_quotes.evidence: We did a lot of record dates in the ‘50s with Marty Paich... Marty Paich I love. I don’t know if we ever did any movies, we did a lot of recordings together, Marty Paich.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Sheldon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Ken Poston
about_quotes.evidence: Marty Paich, very difficult taskmaster. But you would read about the things people had written and a lot of times it just didn’t match up with the person that I had gotten to know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ken Poston interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 4, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Strazzeri
about_quotes.evidence: when they merged groups like say Art Pepper on alto and Stu Williamson on trumpet and Bill Perkins on tenor and Frank Rosolino on trombone, and Marty Paich on piano and so forth
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Strazzeri interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Broadbent
about_quotes.evidence: I would listen. And then eventually I got to be on the road with Natalie Cole... packing up the scores of Johnny Mandel, Marty Paich... it was spelled out for me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Broadbent interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 26, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Yeah, I think probably that one and the two with Marty Paich with the Dek-Tette. [INTERVIEWEE] Oh the Dek-Tette stuff yeah.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
